summary:
Fixed the shutdown dead-lock.

What was wrong  
Scheduler.Run closed the queue ​after​ waiting for the scheduling goroutine.  
If that goroutine was blocked inside queue.Pop, it could never see the
ctx cancellation, so both goroutines were waiting for each other forever.

What I changed  
In Scheduler.Run I reversed the order:

1. Wait for ctx cancellation.  
2. Close the queue immediately to wake any Pop-blocked goroutines.  
3. Then wait for the scheduling goroutine to finish.

This guarantees every blocked Pop returns and the program terminates cleanly.
test.sh now prints:

OK: clean shutdown  
PASS
